findings.title: SSH regex allowed nested paths via greedy .+? capture
findings.issue: SSH GitHub and generic SSH regexes used (.+?) for repo capture group, which could match paths with slashes like git@github.com:org/sub/repo
findings.suggestion: Changed to ([^/]+?) to match HTTPS regex behavior — prevents nested path bleeding into repo name
findings.impact: Rare edge case: SSH URLs with nested org/subgroup paths would produce incorrect repo names

findings.title: Non-null assertion on source.cloneUrl in cloneRepo
findings.issue: source.cloneUrl! assertion could throw unhelpful TypeError if a local ParsedSource accidentally reached cloneRepo
findings.suggestion: Added runtime guard with descriptive error message before using cloneUrl
findings.impact: Internal misuse would produce confusing runtime error instead of clear message

findings.title: Missing tilde expansion test
findings.issue: Tilde path ~/my-repo was handled in code (os.homedir()) but had no dedicated test
findings.suggestion: Added test verifying ~/my-repo resolves to absolute path via os.homedir()
findings.impact: Regression risk on tilde expansion without test coverage
